flashcards.front: State the Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages.
flashcards.back: If L is a CFL, there exists a pumping length p such that any string sвИИL with |s|вЙ•p can be written as s=uvwxy where |vwx|вЙ§p and vxвЙ†ќµ. Moreover, for every iвЙ•0, uv^i w x^i y is also in L.

flashcards.front: What do the five components u, v, w, x, y represent in the CFL pumping lemma?
flashcards.back: They partition a long string s: u and y are the unchanged left and right contexts, v and x are the substrings that get pumped (repeated or deleted), and w is the middle part that stays fixed.

flashcards.front: What are the two main constraints on the decomposition s=uvwxy in the CFL pumping lemma?
flashcards.back: First, |vwx|вЙ§p, guaranteeing the pumped part lies within the first p symbols. Second, at least one of v or x is nonвАСempty (vxвЙ†ќµ), so the decomposition truly repeats a nonвАСtrivial substring.

flashcards.front: Outline the proof technique for showing a language is not contextвАСfree using the pumping lemma.
flashcards.back: Assume L is CFL, pick a sufficiently long string sвИИL, write s=uvwxy satisfying the lemmaвАЩs conditions, then choose an i (usually 0 or 2) that makes uv^i w x^i y leave L; the contradiction proves L is not CFL.

flashcards.front: Why is it incorrect to write the pumping lemma for CFL as s=uvxyz (six symbols) instead of s=uvwxy?
flashcards.back: The CFL lemma uses five symbols (u,v,w,x,y); using six conflates it with the regularвАСlanguage lemma and violates the required constraints, leading to false proofs.

flashcards.front: Use the pumping lemma to show L={a^n b^n c^n | nвЙ•0} is not contextвАСfree.
flashcards.back: Assume CFL, pick s=a^p b^p c^p, decompose as uvwxy with |vwx|вЙ§p; because vwx cannot contain all three letter blocks, pumping i=0 or 2 yields a string with mismatched counts of a, b, or c, contradicting membership in L.

flashcards.front: Which of the following languages is contextвАСfree? (i) {ww^R | wвИИ{a,b}*} (ii) {a^i b^j c^k | i=j or j=k} (iii) {a^n b^n c^n | nвЙ•0}.
flashcards.back: Only (i) {ww^R} is contextвАСfree (it can be generated by a pushdown automaton); languages (ii) and (iii) are not CFL and can be disproved with the pumping lemma.

flashcards.front: How does the pumping lemma for CFL arise from properties of parse trees?
flashcards.back: In CNF, any derivation tree of depth вЙ• p contains a path of length вЙ• p; the repeated subtrees correspond to substrings v and x that can be pumped, giving the lemmaвАЩs decomposition.
